title: poa angustifolia var. nemoralis
intro: Poa angustifolia var. nemoralis, also known as mountain bluegrass, is a variety of the perennial grass Poa angustifolia that grows in mountainous regions. It is primarily used as a medicinal herb, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have a soothing effect on the digestive system. Mountain bluegrass can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
